title: Work within a laboratory or field workplace (Induction)
application: This unit covers the induction of an employee into scientific or technical work within a laboratory or field workplace. Applicable to samplers, testers, production operators and field assistants across all industry sectors.
competent_person_statement: A person competent in this unit demonstrates a consistent record of working within workplace structure and culture, in accordance with workplace agreements and legislative requirements. They accept responsibility for the quality of their own work, work to a routine calendar of events, organise and prioritise assigned tasks without ongoing direction, and actively participate in training and development. They comply with all WHS policies and legislation, use PPE correctly, perform work in an environmentally responsible manner, communicate effectively with colleagues and supervisors, seek clarification when needed, identify and report improvement opportunities, and consult with their supervisor to plan ongoing training and career development.

knowledge_requirements.text: Workplace organisational structure, culture, departments, and business ethics
Workplace quality systems and documentation including SOPs, work instructions, records management, and quality improvement processes
WHS legislation, codes of practice, and regulatory requirements applicable to laboratory or field environments
Types of hazards and risks encountered in scientific or technical workplaces and appropriate control measures
Environmental legislation, workplace environmental policies, and responsible environmental practices
Types of PPE used in laboratory or field environments, selection criteria, correct use, and maintenance
Workplace communication methods, protocols, and escalation procedures
Principles of workload planning, task prioritisation, and time management in a scientific or technical workplace
Training and career development processes and how to access them within the workplace

skill_requirements.text: Read and interpret workplace documents including SOPs, work instructions, safety data sheets, and quality records
Follow multi-step technical instructions accurately and consistently without compromising safety or quality
Complete workplace records and documentation accurately and within required timeframes
Access and use workplace information systems such as LIMS, intranet databases, and electronic records
Select, don, doff, and maintain personal protective equipment appropriate to specific laboratory or field tasks
Identify workplace hazards, assess risk, and report through appropriate channels
Maintain own work area and equipment in a safe, clean, and organised condition
Communicate clearly and professionally in written and verbal forms with team members and supervisors
Plan, organise, and prioritise daily work tasks to meet schedules and quality requirements
Actively participate in workplace training activities and apply new learning to work tasks

evidence_guide.title: Laboratory map or floor plan
evidence_guide.description: A copy of your Emergency Evacuation Plan map, or a hand-drawn map of your laboratory and/or workplace.
acceptable_forms: Hand-drawn mud map of your lab
Site plan showing your work area

evidence_guide.title: Organisational chart
evidence_guide.description: Your laboratory, department, or full business structure showing reporting lines.
acceptable_forms: Hand-drawn structure showing your reporting line

evidence_guide.title: Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)
evidence_guide.description: An SOP or test method you follow routinely in your job role.
acceptable_forms: A test method SOP you use daily
A procedure document with your role referenced

evidence_guide.title: Record of improvement communication
evidence_guide.description: A record showing you reported an improvement opportunity to your supervisor.
acceptable_forms: Email to supervisor about an improvement
Verbal record — signed by all parties

evidence_guide.title: WHS and Environmental Policy
evidence_guide.description: A copy of your workplace WHS Policy or Environmental Management Procedure.

evidence_guide.title: Hazard report or supervisor communication
evidence_guide.description: A record of reporting a hazard or requesting additional resources.
acceptable_forms: Verbal record — signed by all parties

evidence_guide.title: Evidence of work over time
evidence_guide.description: Evidence demonstrating how you assess and prioritise your workload.

evidence_guide.title: Performance review or training request
evidence_guide.description: Your latest performance review showing training discussions, or a request for this course.

evidence_guide.title: Workplace Confidentiality or Privacy Policy
evidence_guide.description: A copy of your workplace Confidentiality or Privacy Policy.

evidence_guide.title: Third Party Report — Workplace Supervisor (Required)
evidence_guide.description: Completed by your direct supervisor — the strongest evidence item for RPL. Provides independent confirmation of your performance across all five elements.
acceptable_forms: Download the Third Party Report template
Ask your supervisor to complete and sign it
